metadata.title: Crown Him with Many Crowns
metadata.subtitle: Matthew Bridges (1851) / Godfrey Thring (1874)
metadata.x_pd_basis: Bridges (d. 1894), Thring (d. 1903), and Elvey (d. 1893) all died > 120 years ago

chordpro:
{title: Crown Him with Many Crowns}
{subtitle: Bridges (1851) / Thring (1874) — Elvey "Diademata" tune, 1868}
{key: D}
{time: 4/4}
{tempo: 100}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[D]Crown Him with [A]many [D]crowns
[Bm]The Lamb up[A]on His [D]throne
[D]Hark! How the [G]heavenly [D]anthem drowns
[Em]All [A]music but its [D]own
[D]Awake, my [G]soul, and [D]sing
[Bm]Of Him who [A]died for [D]thee
[D]And hail Him [G]as thy [D]matchless King
[Em]Through [A]all eter[D]nity
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[D]Crown Him the [A]Lord of [D]life
[Bm]Who triumphed [A]o'er the [D]grave
[D]And rose vic[G]torious [D]in the strife
[Em]For [A]those He came to [D]save
[D]His glories [G]now we [D]sing
[Bm]Who died and [A]rose on [D]high
[D]Who died e[G]ternal [D]life to bring
[Em]And [A]lives that death may [D]die
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 3}
[D]Crown Him the [A]Lord of [D]love
[Bm]Behold His [A]hands and [D]side
[D]Rich wounds, yet [G]visible [D]above
[Em]In [A]beauty glori[D]fied
[D]No angel [G]in the [D]sky
[Bm]Can fully [A]bear that [D]sight
[D]But downward [G]bends his [D]wondering eye
[Em]At [A]mysteries so [D]bright
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 4}
[D]Crown Him the [A]Lord of [D]years
[Bm]The Potentate [A]of [D]time
[D]Creator [G]of the [D]rolling spheres
[Em]In[A]effably sub[D]lime
[D]All hail, Re[G]deemer, [D]hail!
[Bm]For Thou hast [A]died for [D]me
[D]Thy praise and [G]glory [D]shall not fail
[Em]Through[A]out eter[D]nity
{end_of_verse}
